0.title: AI is generating your Core Plan
0.description:
It will take just a moment. 
We'll briefly introduce how to use DocsHunt.

1.title: Auto-generation + Direct editing
1.description: AI creates the framework, and you can edit the details. Think of it as an assistant writing your business plan.

2.title: Export to any format
2.description: The completed Core Plan can be exported in various formats for proposals, applications, or internal sharing.

3.title: Company (team) info is up to you
3.description:
AI does not generate the 7. Company/Team section.
Please write your own story and information.

document.title: Create New Core Plan
document.emptyMethodDescription: You can start writing directly in an empty document.
document.guideMethodDescription: Answer a few questions to draft your business plan.

menuNavigation.deleteTitle: Are you sure you want to delete this Core Plan?
menuNavigation.deleteDescription: This action cannot be undone. Pressing the 'Delete Core Plan' button will permanently remove all of its content.

description.heading1: Big section heading. Displayed in the left menu.
description.heading2: Medium section heading. Displayed in the left menu.
description.heading3: Small section heading. Displayed in the left menu.
description.toggleableHeading1: Hide content inside a large heading.
description.toggleableHeading2: Hide content inside a medium heading.
description.toggleableHeading3: Hide content inside a small heading.
description.image: Upload an image or embed with a link.
description.timeline: Visualize the flow of events over time.
description.tamSamSom: Visualize nested structures such as TAM/SAM/SOM.
description.concentricCirclesDiagram: Visualize nested structures such as TAM/SAM/SOM.
description.positioningMap: Visualize product positions on two axes.
description.mermaid: Visualize a process in sequential shapes.
description.barChart: Compare categorical data using bar lengths.
description.lineChart: Express changes or trends over time with lines.
description.composedChart: Analyze with a combination of bars and lines.
description.donutChart: Visualize proportions of items in a donut shape.

mainPage.title: Write your item description and generate a business plan
mainPage.titleMobile:
Write your item description and
generate a business plan
mainPage.example:
[Writing Example]
- Item Name: DocsHunt 
- Item Description: A solution that generates business plan drafts with AI and converts them to your desired format 
- Target Customers: Startup CEOs and practitioners who struggle with business plan writing 
- Problem Solved: Inefficiency in document writing due to varying business plan formats
mainPage.detailDescription: If you already have a business plan, you can also convert it to your desired format.

promo.description: Subscribe now to lock in the launch special price for life as long as you stay subscribed.

mainPrompt.placeholder: What kind of business are you in? Please enter the name and introduction of the item, the target customer, and the problem you want to solve as an example. The more specifically you tell them, the more accurate the AI generates a business plan.
mainPrompt.fileDescription: The content of added files will be reflected in the document
mainPrompt.maxFilesLimit: You can upload up to 3 files only
mainPrompt.calculatingCredit: Calculating credits. Please wait a moment.

toast.referenceFileTooLarge: The reference file is too large.
toast.genericError: An error occurred while creating the document.

xmlCharacterLimitModal.description: One of the document files (hwp, hwpx, docx) contains too much content.
xmlCharacterLimitModal.convertToPdf: Convert it to PDF and try again.
xmlCharacterLimitModal.reduceContent: Please reduce the content and try again.

detailInput.title:
You can create a business plan
by just entering the item name and description
detailInput.optionalTitle: The following are optional input fields
itemIntroductionTooltip: Customer-Problem-Solution-Value formula: `[Which customers] solve [what problem] through [what method] to provide [what value].`
Use numbers: If possible, use numbers to show specific benefits. (e.g., 'in 10 minutes', '30% cost reduction')
Use simple words: Explain in simple words that even elementary school students can understand, rather than technical terms.

businessStory.label: Business Story
businessStory.placeholder: Founded by Kim Sung-woo who experienced the inefficiency of repetitive document creation firsthand. Validated real needs through experience of automatically generating over 12,000 business plans. Discovered huge opportunities in the global market
tooltip: Start from the founder's 'real experience' of why this business was started.

Structure the story in this order: `[Problem occurrence (pain)] → [Efforts and failures to solve] → [Key insight/idea discovery] → [Our solution] → [Future we dream of (vision)]` to increase engagement.
Start with the founder's story, but ultimately explain how this business will solve 'customer problems' and change 'customer lives'.

marketAndStrategy.label: Market Analysis & Strategy
marketAndStrategy.placeholder:
Primary target (Korea): MVP validation and initial funding
• Startups, consultants, AC/VC (specialized in government support programs)
• Core target (North America): Canada: September 2025 test market, US: Full-scale entry after Canada success
• Startups (Business Plan, Grant Applications), consulting companies, SMBs, Enterprise
• Market size: North American market $9.1 billion by 2032 (44% of total $20.7 billion)
tooltip: Instead of 'women in their 20s', specify customers like 'Kim Min-ji, a 28-year-old office worker living in Seoul who is interested in self-development after work but lacks time' to make customers more concrete.
List each competitor and compare them using the same criteria. This will reveal our unique position.

Instead of 'we'll do SNS marketing', present specific action plans and target numbers like 'collaborate with 10 fashion influencers for women in their 2030s on Instagram for the first 3 months to conduct group purchases and secure 1,000 initial customers through this'.

revenueAndFinancial.label: Revenue & Financial Plan
revenueAndFinancial.placeholder:
Sep-Dec 2025: Canada test (target: 100 Canadian users) 2026: Full Canada entry + US test → Revenue 1.71 billion KRW
2027: Full North America expansion → Revenue 7.21 billion KRW 2028: Global expansion → Revenue 17.8 billion KRW
Break-even point: Achieved in 36 months (monthly revenue 280M KRW vs operating cost 50M KRW)
tooltip: Clearly define how you will make money: subscription fees, brokerage fees, advertising revenue, product sales, etc. (e.g., basic features are free, premium features have a monthly subscription fee of 9,900 KRW)
Dividing costs into fixed costs (personnel costs, rent, etc.) and variable costs (material costs, advertising costs, etc.) makes forecasting easier.

Instead of 'we'll make 1 billion next year', estimate sales by building up from the bottom using logical grounds like `(target customers) X (purchase conversion rate) X (average order value) = expected revenue`.

progressAndRoadmap.label: Current Progress & Roadmap
progressAndRoadmap.placeholder:
2026: 1.71 billion KRW (North America 30% + Korea 70%)
2027: 7.21 billion KRW (North America 70% + Korea 30%)
2028: 17.8 billion KRW (North America 80% + Others 20%)

Revenue model: SaaS subscription Starter: $39/month (Korea 39,000 KRW/month), Pro: $99/month (Korea 99,000 KRW/month), Enterprise: Custom pricing
tooltip: It's good to show plans step by step in a list format according to the flow of time.

Focus on important milestones that determine the direction of the business, such as 'MVP launch', 'securing 1,000 beta testers', 'achieving break-even point', rather than minor tasks like 'starting coding'.
It's good to show current progress based on achievements so far (past), and present future plans (1 year, 3 years) based on this.

teamInfo.label: Team Information
teamInfo.placeholder: Please describe your team members and their roles
tooltip: Instead of simply 'worked at OO company', write about achievements directly related to this business, such as 'led XX project at OO company and achieved 150% revenue growth'.

Explain how the capabilities each team member has (development, marketing, planning, etc.) complement each other to create synergy. It's important to show that our team has all the core capabilities needed for the business.

placeholders.name: e.g., John Doe
placeholders.skills: e.g., PhD in Computer Science, 10+ years experience in AI

completion.message1:
Your business plan is complete!

Please review the completed business plan and enter any modifications in the input field below. DocsHunt AI will make the changes for you right away.
completion.message2: If no modifications are needed, you can download the file by clicking the blue "Export" button in the top right corner.

toast.progress: The document is still being generated. You can download it after completion.
